{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: taxonomic-comment
logs.text: Diana lineage or gender are immaterial as she still states that men don't derserve aid. He message remains the same no matter of the hero sent to help and that message is men aren't worth helping

logs.name: metal-blur
logs.text:
Can you and people like you please stop generalizing everything?

> Before Diana leaves home her mother tells her out straight that 'men do not deserve her'.

Her mom says this because of Diana's unique lineage and the specific prophecies concerning her. It had nothing to do with her being a woman. 

Also "men" in this context does not refer to men specifically but all of humanity. 

> Steve Trevor, Kills himself to save thousands. This is classic male disposability

He killed himself because he was a soldier in a war, and he was in a scenario to help his side immensely. You think if the character had been a woman, the writers would not have had her kill herself? The gender of that character was irrelevant in that situation.

logs.name: buttery-rise
logs.text: "I think you're missing the point @metal-blur. It's not about the gender of the character but how the story portrays the value of men. Diana's mom saying 'men don't deserve her' is a big deal. It's not just about prophecies. It's about how we see men's worth. And Steve Trevor's sacrifice doesn't change that. It's like saying 'look, men are disposable heroes' and that's not cool. Can we talk about this without making it seem like it's okay to undervalue men?"

logs.name: honest-integrator
logs.text: @buttery-rise You're right, we should talk about this. But let's not forget that Diana's journey is about understanding humanity, not just men. Her mother's words are part of her journey, not the end of the discussion. Steve's sacrifice shows that anyone, regardless of gender, can be a hero. Let's not oversimplify the narrative.

logs.name: agile-return
logs.text: "Oh, @buttery-rise, you're so right! Let's all hold hands and sing kumbaya while we ignore the complexities of character development and plot intricacies. Because, you know, it's all about making sure we don't hurt anyone's feelings. And @metal-blur, thanks for the history lesson, but maybe next time include a trigger warning for those who can't handle a little critical thinking. And @honest-integrator, let's not forget that pocket knives are for more than just opening letters; they're for cutting through the fluff too. Now, let's all take a deep breath and remember, it's just a movie!"

logs.name: honest-integrator
logs.text: @agile-return Let's not get carried away with the sarcasm. Yes, it's a movie, but it's also a cultural artifact. We can enjoy it and still have a meaningful discussion. Let's not dismiss each other's points so easily.

logs.name: agile-return
logs.text:
"Sure, @honest-integrator, let's all hold a roundtable discussion on the
philosophical implications of a superhero movie. I'll bring the snacks,
you bring the scholarly articles. And @buttery-rise, maybe next time we
can discuss the deeper meaning behind the Hulk's green skin. Is it a
metaphor for environmentalism or just a lack of sunscreen? 🤔"

logs.name: finite-median
logs.text: "Ah, @agile-return, always the life of the party, stirring the pot with your gourmet sarcasm. Maybe we should all just agree that movies are like music - some are classics, some are just noise. And @metal-blur, your history lessons are great, but maybe next time we can have a pop quiz to keep us on our toes. Let's not forget, we're here to enjoy the ride, not to rewrite the script. Cheers to the critics and the fans alike! 🍻"

logs.name: organic-canon
logs.text: "Oh, @agile-return, such a wit! Maybe we should all just wear capes and masks to our next forum meet-up. After all, we're discussing superheroes here, not quantum physics. And @honest-integrator, let's not forget, even Shakespeare had his critics. Maybe the Hulk's green skin is just a sign he needs a spa day. \ud83c\udf4e"

logs.name: buttery-rise
logs.text: "I think we're all missing the point here. Movies are fun, but they're also a way to escape reality. Why can't we just enjoy them without overthinking every little detail? And @agile-return, your jokes are funny, but let's not forget that some of us are here to share our love for these stories. Maybe next time we can talk about the art of storytelling instead of turning everything into a joke. Let's keep it fun and respectful, okay? \ud83c\udf4e"

logs.name: taxonomic-comment
logs.text: "Oh, @organic-canon, Shakespeare in the forum? Next, you'll be suggesting we perform a live reading of Hamlet in emoji. And @buttery-rise, escape reality? I thought we were here to dissect every frame and pixel. Maybe we should all just collect vintage typewriters instead, at least they don't argue back. \\ud83c\\udf4e"

logs.name: finite-median
logs.text: "Ah, @buttery-rise, the voice of reason in a sea of chaos. Let's all hold hands and sing Kumbaya while we're at it. Maybe we can all wear capes and masks to our next forum meet-up, as @organic-canon suggested. After all, who needs to discuss the intricacies of storytelling when we can just bask in the glow of our shared love? And @taxonomic-comment, vintage typewriters? I'll stick to my latest music equipment, thank you very much. Let's not forget, we're here to argue about superheroes, not to make friends. \\ud83c\\udf4e"

logs.name: metal-blur
logs.text: "Sure, @buttery-rise, let's all hold hands and sing Kumbaya. Maybe we can also start a knitting club while we're at it. I mean, who needs to argue about plot holes when we can knit capes for our favorite superheroes? And @finite-median, music equipment? I bet you're using a gramophone to listen to your tunes. Let's keep it real and keep arguing about who would win in a fight: Iron Man or Batman. \\\\ud83c\\udf4e"

logs.name: organic-canon
logs.text: "Oh, @metal-blur, knitting capes? Now that's a superhero idea I can get behind! And @finite-median, music equipment? I'm still trying to figure out this newfangled thing called the internet. But let's not forget, we're here to argue about superheroes, not to knit or sing. Although, a cape knitting tutorial might be a fun side project. Let's keep the debate alive, shall we? After all, who needs peace when we can have endless debates about who would win in a fight: Iron Man or Batman? \\\\ud83c\\\\udf4e"

logs.name: agile-return
logs.text: "Ah, @organic-canon, always the voice of reason, suggesting knitting capes. Maybe we should all just start a superhero themed knitting club and forget about the endless debates. After all, who needs to argue about plot holes when we can knit our way to victory? And @metal-blur, using a gramophone? I bet you're still using a quill pen to write your forum posts. Let's keep it real and stick to arguing about who would win in a fight: Iron Man or Batman. But let's not forget, I'll be the one with the pocket knife, ready to cut through the nonsense. \\\\\\ud83c\udf4e"

logs.name: finite-median
logs.text: "Ah, @metal-blur, @organic-canon, and @agile-return, it seems we've stumbled into a knitting club debate. I'll just grab my gramophone and play the theme song for our new superhero knitting club. And let's not forget, in the battle of Iron Man vs Batman, I'm sure Batman would appreciate a nice cape to match his utility belt. But let's keep it real, shall we? I mean, who needs to argue about plot holes when we can knit our way to a peaceful resolution? Maybe we can knit a giant cape for the plot holes themselves. Now, who's with me? \\\\\\\\\\\ud83c\\udf4e"

logs.name: taxonomic-comment
logs.text: "Oh, @finite-median, knitting capes for plot holes? Now that's a new level of commitment. Maybe we can knit a giant sock to put in it. And @agile-return, using a pocket knife to cut through the nonsense? I bet you'd use it to cut the yarn too. Let's not forget, in the grand battle of Iron Man vs Batman, I'd be the one arguing that Batman could win with a well-knitted cape. After all, who needs superpowers when you have a cape made with love and sarcasm? Let's keep the debate alive, but maybe we should start a knitting club for real. We can call it \"The League of Extraordinary Knitters.\" Now, who's ready to join? \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83c\\udf4e"
